name: Alto Bernesga Biosphere Reserve Loop
name_original: La Tercia del Camino – circular al embalse de Casares desde La Pola de Gordón
description: This difficult 32.5-mile touring cycling route from La Pola de Gordón to the Casares reservoir offers stunning views and challenging terrain
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around La Pola De Gordón are situated within the Alto Bernesga Biosphere Reserve, part of the Cantabrian Mountains in Castilla y León. The region features diverse landscapes, including river valleys carved by the Bernesga and Torío rivers, limestone gorges, and ancient beech forests. This area offers a variety of terrain, from gentle paths along riverbanks to challenging ascents through mountainous areas, suitable for different cycling preferences. Best touring cycling routes around La Pola De Gordón The most popular touring cycling route is Alto Bernesga Biosphere Reserve Loop, a 32.5 miles (52.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 51 minutes to complete. This route offers extensive views within the UNESCO-recognized biosphere reserve. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is La Pola de Gordón Downhill Trail, a difficult 21.6 miles (34.8 km) path. It features varied mountain scenery with sections of forest tracks and descents. Local touring cyclists also love the Ermita del Buen Suceso – La Muria mountain pass loop from Peredilla, a 12.0 miles (19.4 km) trail leading through mountain passes and rural landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 50 minutes. Touring cycling around La Pola De Gordón is defined by the Cantabrian Mountains, river valleys, and the Alto Bernesga Biosphere Reserve. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate rides to more demanding routes with significant elevation gain. The routes in La Pola De Gordón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 250 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore La Pola De Gordón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in La Pola de Gordón?
A: La Pola de Gordón offers a wide selection of touring cycling routes, with over 30 options available. These routes cater to various skill levels, from moderate rides to more demanding journeys through the region's diverse terrain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in La Pola de Gordón?
A: The terrain in La Pola de Gordón is highly varied, reflecting its location within the Alto Bernesga Biosphere Reserve and the Cantabrian Mountains. You can expect everything from gentle paths along riverbanks, such as the Río Bernesga, to challenging ascents through mountainous areas. The landscape includes river valleys, impressive limestone gorges like Los Calderones and Vegacervera, and ancient beech forests such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5235085" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ciñera Beech Forest&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or those seeking an easier ride?
A: While many routes in La Pola de Gordón are challenging, there are options for less experienced cyclists. The region offers a few easy and moderate routes. For instance, the circular route Pola – La Vid – Santa Lucía – Ciñera – Pola is a 25 km ride with gentle terrain and low traffic, ideal for a calm experience. You can find a moderate route like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24636220" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guzmán del Bueno Fountain – Bridge of the Lions loop from La Robla&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 57.7 km with less elevation gain.

Q: What are some of the more challenging touring cycling routes in the area?
A: For experienced touring cyclists, La Pola de Gordón presents numerous demanding routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1037344610/alto-bernesga-biosphere-reserve-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto Bernesga Biosphere Reserve Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 52.3 km trail with significant elevation gain, offering extensive views. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1036911985/la-pola-de-gordon-downhill-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Pola de Gordón Downhill Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a 34.8 km path featuring varied mountain scenery and forest tracks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38157711" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita del Buen Suceso – La Muria mountain pass loop from Peredilla&lt;/a&gt; is also a difficult 19.4 km route through mountain passes.

Q: What natural features can I explore while touring cycling in La Pola de Gordón?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can cycle through the UNESCO-recognized Alto Bernesga Biosphere Reserve, home to diverse flora and fauna. Don't miss the enchanting &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5235085" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ciñera Beech Forest&lt;/a&gt;, known for its ancient trees, or the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4073598" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Calderones Gorge&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3541942" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vegacervera Gorge&lt;/a&gt;, carved by the Torío River. The tranquil Río Bernesga also meanders through the area, offering peaceful spots.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or points of interest along the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, the area combines natural beauty with cultural and historical interest. You can explore traditional towns that reflect the region's mining and railway heritage. Geological enthusiasts will appreciate the 'Punto de Interés Geológico Internacional 'Formación Huergas - La Vid''. Additionally, you might encounter religious sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5558242" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Virgin of Manadero in the Cave of the Doves&lt;/a&gt; or various mountain peaks such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/859306" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz de San Salvador&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5556607" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Fontañán Summit&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find routes with panoramic viewpoints in La Pola de Gordón?
A: Absolutely. For breathtaking panoramic views, consider routes that ascend to higher elevations. The ascent to Cueto San Mateo offers some of the best vistas in northern León. Similarly, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5556607" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Fontañán Summit&lt;/a&gt; provides expansive views of the surrounding Cantabrian Mountains and the Biosphere Reserve.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes available in La Pola de Gordón?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in La Pola de Gordón are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1037344610/alto-bernesga-biosphere-reserve-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto Bernesga Biosphere Reserve Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38157711" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita del Buen Suceso – La Muria mountain pass loop from Peredilla&lt;/a&gt;. The road cycling route Pola – La Vid – Santa Lucía – Ciñera – Pola is also a circular option.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in La Pola de Gordón?
A: The region is beautiful year-round, but autumn is particularly spectacular for cycling, especially when visiting areas like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5235085" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ciñera Beech Forest&lt;/a&gt;, as the colors are vibrant. Spring and summer also offer pleasant conditions for exploring the diverse landscapes, with warmer temperatures and lush greenery.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in La Pola de Gordón?
A: The touring cycling routes in La Pola de Gordón are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars. Cyclists frequently praise the diverse mountain scenery, the quiet forest tracks, and the opportunity to explore the stunning Alto Bernesga Biosphere Reserve. The variety of routes, from gentle rides to challenging ascents, is also a highlight for many.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling options in La Pola de Gordón?
A: While many routes are geared towards more experienced cyclists, families can enjoy the gentler sections along the Río Bernesga or the less trafficked road cycling routes. The path to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5235085" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Faedo de Ciñera&lt;/a&gt;, though primarily a hiking trail, is short and accessible, making it an ideal family excursion that can be combined with a gentle cycle nearby.

Q: Is public transport available to access touring cycling routes in La Pola de Gordón?
A: La Pola de Gordón is a municipality within the Alto Bernesga Biosphere Reserve. While specific public transport options directly to trailheads may vary, the town itself serves as a central point. It's advisable to check local bus schedules or regional train services that connect to La Pola de Gordón, from where many routes are accessible.

Q: Where can I find parking for touring cycling routes in La Pola de Gordón?
A: La Pola de Gordón, as a traditional town, typically offers various parking options, especially in and around the town center. Many routes start from or pass through the town, making it a convenient base. For specific trailheads outside the main town, it's recommended to check local signage or route descriptions for designated parking areas.
tips: It is a somewhat nondescript hermitage that could not be more attached to the road. Its entrance is covered and can serve as an improvised shelter in the event of a downpour.

Its main square, Plaza de la Constitución, offers a postcard view with its meticulously pruned trees and the partially whitewashed church tower, in a contrast also present in most houses. An adorable fact about this mining town is that in a nearby copper and cobalt deposit that began to be exploited in 1916, a unique mineral was found in the world that was designated as "villamaninite", since it was in this town where the minerals from the mine in the trains.

Cultural and Religious Heritage: La Pola de Gordón has a number of historic churches and chapels, such as the Iglesia Nuestra Señora del Buen Suceso and the Ermita de Nuestra Señora de Celada, which are worth a visit for their architecture and history.

faqs.question: Is this touring bicycle route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is categorized as "difficult" and requires very good physical fitness. Cyclists should expect challenging terrain, including unpaved sections where dismounting and pushing your bicycle may be necessary.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: The route typically takes around 3 hours and 50 minutes to complete by touring bicycle, covering a distance of approximately 52.3 km.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the La Tercia del Camino route?
faqs.answer: While mostly paved, this route includes unpaved and challenging segments. Be prepared for varied surfaces, and you might need to dismount and push your bicycle in some sections due to the demanding nature of the trail.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the La Tercia del Camino route in La Pola de Gordón?
faqs.answer: You can typically find parking in the vicinity of La Pola de Gordón Town Center, which serves as the starting point for this circular route. Specific parking areas may vary, so it's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers diverse scenery and points of interest. You'll pass by the Hermitage of the Virgin of the Light of Buiza and through Plaza de la Constitución, Villamanín. Expect magnificent views of the Crestellina mountain range and Monte del Duque as you approach the Casares reservoir. The area is also rich in Mediterranean forest vegetation and offers opportunities to spot griffon vultures.

faqs.question: Is this route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed on trails in this region, given the route's difficulty, length, and potential for challenging terrain, it might not be ideal for all dogs, especially those not accustomed to long, strenuous bicycle rides. Always keep your dog on a leash and ensure they are well-hydrated.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is typically during spring or autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be very hot, and winter may bring adverse weather conditions, making some sections difficult or unsafe. Always check the local weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this area?
faqs.answer: This route is located within the Reserva de la Biosfera del Alto Bernesga. Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on public trails within the Biosphere Reserve. However, always respect local regulations, stay on marked paths, and practice Leave No Trace principles to protect the natural environment.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other notable paths. You will find yourself on parts of the Vía de la Plata (28% of the route) and the Camino del Salvador (16% of the route). Smaller segments also intersect with Sendero de las Tres Marías, Calleja Oscura, Camino del Valle, and Sendero en Arroyo del Villar.

faqs.question: What wildlife can I expect to see along the route?
faqs.answer: The area is known for its rich biodiversity. Keep an eye out for griffon vultures, which have a significant colony in the Crestellina mountain range. The Mediterranean forest vegetation also supports various other bird species and small mammals.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or water sources along the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through La Pola de Gordón Town Center and Villamanín, where you may find cafes, shops, and opportunities to refill water. However, it's always recommended to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially for the more remote sections of the trail.
